spec_match.rationale: The demo now leaves anchor-name: --target declared on every row and uses anchor-scope on each list to show scoped local lookups versus a leaking page-level lookup.

interactive.rationale: The scope toggle and measurement button update the rendered anchor positions and diagnostic readout without mutating anchor-name inline.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now describes the actual CSS behavior: local list badges can resolve scoped names, while an outside global probe is prevented from leaking to the final matching row when scope is enabled.

edge_cases.rationale: Both the scoped case and the without-scope failure are visible, and unsupported browsers get explicit feature-detection badges plus anchor() fallback positioning.

references.rationale: The page includes the ChromeStatus entry for feature 5094192052436992 and the CSS Anchor Positioning anchor-scope spec section.

design_system.rationale: The page uses design-system variables, explicit button colors, stable responsive grids, no raw color literals, and no inline event handlers or style mutations.

summary: The anchor-scope concept now demonstrates the CSS-only scoping contrast directly: row anchors stay permanent, list-local badges resolve inside their scopes, and the global probe exposes the final-row leak when scope is disabled.
